claimedStatus: Server-side. Addressed by createScopedRepository(profileId) + parent-chain WHERE filters.
evidence:
The IDOR is closed by a two-layer server-side defense in apps/api/src/routes/profiles.ts and services/profile.ts.

Route gate (profiles.ts:161-168, PATCH /profiles/:id):
```
const activeProfileId = c.get('profileId');
const profileMeta = c.get('profileMeta');
if (profileMeta?.isOwner !== true && id !== activeProfileId) {
  return apiError(c, 403, ERROR_CODES.FORBIDDEN,
    'Only the account owner can update other profiles.');
}
```
A non-owner can only edit their own profile (self-id), never a sibling. The same gate guards PATCH /profiles/:id/app-context (profiles.ts:118-125).

Service scoping (services/profile.ts:588-594, updateProfile):
```
.where(and(
  eq(profiles.id, profileId),
  eq(profiles.accountId, accountId),
  isNull(profiles.archivedAt),
))
```
The WHERE pins both profileId AND accountId, so even if the route gate were bypassed, a cross-account write returns null (-> 404). updateProfileAppContext (profile.ts:614-620) applies the same account-scoped filter plus an isOwner/age/family-link check.

Break tests exist (red-green regression, as required by the repo's "security fixes require a break test" rule):
- profiles.test.ts:502-542 [BREAK][CR-2026-05-19-H1] "returns 403 when a non-owner edits a sibling profile" — PATCH PROFILE_ID_B while active=PROFILE_ID_A (non-owner) asserts 403, code FORBIDDEN, and updateProfileMock NOT called.
- profiles.test.ts:674-699 same for the app-context route.
- profiles.test.ts:705+ positive companion: non-owner CAN update their own profile.
recommendation: Keep status as Closed/Addressed (server-side), but correct the cited mechanism: the gap is closed by (a) a route-level isOwner + self-id gate in profiles.ts (CR-2026-05-19-H1) and (b) an accountId-scoped WHERE in updateProfile/updateProfileAppContext — NOT by createScopedRepository(profileId) or parent-chain joins (this is a single-table profiles write, not a parent-chain read). Suggested matrix wording: "Closed server-side. PATCH /profiles/:id and /app-context: route gate rejects non-owner sibling edits (profiles.ts:161-168, 118-125); updateProfile WHERE pins accountId (profile.ts:588-594). Break tests: profiles.test.ts:502, 674."

claimedStatus: Closed; AST ratchet excludes object-literal property writes.
evidence:
F6's substance is correct: the analytics `isOwner` read is NOT flagged as a UI gate, and the guard excludes the object-literal analytics writes — but the cited line numbers (649/653/661) are stale.

(1) The owner read is now a single consolidated declaration at apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/subscription.tsx:129 — `const isOwnerProfile = activeProfile?.isOwner === true;` — carrying a leading comment "Account-identity fact: drives analytics and the child-paywall routing decision below." It is emitted as the `is_owner` analytics property at line 137 (`breakdownAnalytics`) and line 145 (`track('subscription_breakdown_mounted', { is_owner: isOwnerProfile, ... })`).

(2) The claimed lines 649/653/661 now point at UNRELATED code — the ChildPaywall `quotaKind` daily-vs-monthly logic: line 648-654 `usage?.dailyLimit != null && usage?.dailyRemainingQuestions === 0 ? 'daily_exceeded' : 'monthly_exceeded'` and the `<ChildPaywall>` return block (655-661). No isOwner involved.

(3) The guard mechanism is apps/mobile/src/lib/navigation-contract-usage-guard.test.ts, not the spec's hypothetical `navigation-contract.guard.test.ts`. Its scanner (line 613-620) emits a `profile-owner-read` finding ONLY for `ts.isPropertyAccessExpression(node)` where `node.name.text === 'isOwner'` — i.e. `.isOwner` accesses. The analytics writes `is_owner: isOwnerProfile` are PropertyAssignment nodes referencing the LOCAL identifier `isOwnerProfile`, so they are NOT counted — exactly matching F6's "AST ratchet excludes object-literal property writes" claim.

(4) subscription.tsx has exactly 3 `.isOwner` property accesses (lines 129, 949 `{member.isOwner`, 953 `!member.isOwner`), matching the guard's pinned `expectedFindings: { 'profile-owner-read': 3 }` (test line 296) with reason "subscription gates UI visibility through the contract, keeps one consolidated owner read for analytics/paywall/V0 fallback, and reads member.isOwner on family-pool rows." subscription.tsx is in the `NON_NAV_DOMAIN_FILES` / `non-nav-domain-read` bucket — explicitly carved out as "must NOT be migrated to the contract." UI gating flows through navigationContract.gates.showBilling (line 130) / showRemoveFamilyMember (line 131), confirming the read is non-UI/analytics-only.
recommendation: Keep F6 as Closed/no-op — the substance holds. Two corrections to the matrix text: (a) update the citation from `subscription.tsx:649, 653, 661` to `subscription.tsx:129` (the consolidated owner read) with analytics emission at lines 137 and 145 — the old lines drifted to unrelated ChildPaywall quotaKind code during a refactor. (b) Replace "AST ratchet excludes object-literal property writes" with the precise mechanism: the implemented guard is `navigation-contract-usage-guard.test.ts`, whose PropertyAccessExpression scanner only counts `.isOwner` member accesses (subscription.tsx pinned at 3, category non-nav-domain-read), so the `is_owner: isOwnerProfile` analytics writes are inherently never flagged. The spec's `navigation-contract.guard.test.ts` AST ratchet was never built under that name.

claimedStatus: Cross-cutting; do not add storage-backed mode persistence without re-reviewing the leak guarantee.
evidence: The leak-guarantee core of F9 holds. apps/mobile/src/lib/app-context.tsx has NO device-local mode persistence — the only ephemeral mode state is `modeOverride` (line 59: `useState<AppMode | null>(null)`), reset to null by the profile-change effect (lines 82-91: `setModeOverride(null)` keyed on activeProfile id/isOwner/birthYear/defaultAppContext/hasFamilyLinks). A grep for setItem/SecureStore in app-context.tsx returns nothing. signOutWithCleanup (apps/mobile/src/lib/sign-out.ts:86-147) clears the profile identity atomically before Clerk signOut: `setActiveProfileId(undefined)` (line 95), `queryClient.clear()` (line 108) wiping the `['profiles']` cache, plus `clearProfileSecureStorageOnSignOut(profileIds)` (line 121) wiping `mentomate_active_profile_id` (profile.ts:107). Since `activeProfile = profiles.find(p => p.id === activeProfileId)` (profile.ts:315-317), clearing both the cache and the saved id severs any cross-account carry-over of mode. BUT the F9 wording "mode is React state only; not persisted" is now stale under V1: derivedMode is computed from the server-persisted `activeProfile.defaultAppContext` (lines 73-75), and setMode persists the choice server-side via `updateAppContext.mutate({ defaultAppContext: nextMode })` (lines 115-119). So mode IS persisted — just profile-scoped on the server, not device-local — and that persistence is covered by the same cleanup (the profile cache is wiped on sign-out). C29/C30 line drifts confirmed: modeOverride at 59 (not 52), reset effect at 82-91 (not 74-84) with an extended dep array.
recommendation: Keep F9 open as a cross-cutting guardrail, but correct the wording. Replace "mode is React state only; not persisted" with: "the mode OVERRIDE is React-state-only (app-context.tsx:59, reset on profile change at 82-91); the baseline mode persists server-side as profiles.defaultAppContext (V1), not device-local. Cross-account leak risk is mitigated because signOutWithCleanup (sign-out.ts:95,108,121) clears activeProfileId + the profiles cache + the saved-id SecureStore key atomically before Clerk signOut." Retain the actionable note: do not add DEVICE-LOCAL (SecureStore/AsyncStorage) mode persistence without re-reviewing this guarantee — server-scoped persistence is already in place and safe. Update C29 cite to line 59 (final mode useMemo 93-97) and C30 cite to lines 82-91.

evidence:
F10's substance holds: the nav contract treats Pro identically to Family at the navigation layer, and Pro/Family divergence is a product/billing decision realized outside navigation.

1) navigation-contract.ts carries tier as an INPUT but never branches pro-vs-family. NavigationSubscriptionContext (apps/mobile/src/lib/navigation-contract.ts:65-70) has `tier: SubscriptionTier | null` and `effectiveAccessTier: SubscriptionTier | null` (SubscriptionTier includes 'pro'), so the dimension is present/forward-compatible. But the only consumption in the contract body is access-PRESENCE, not tier IDENTITY:
- L220-221: `if (context.subscription.status !== 'ready') return false; return context.subscription.effectiveAccessTier !== null;`
- L260: `const subscriptionReady = context.subscription.status === 'ready';`
No `=== 'pro'` / `=== 'family'` discrimination exists anywhere in the contract's gate/shape/intent logic. Shape/intent keys off `familyCapable` / `appContext === 'family'` / `defaultAppContext` (L256, L303-317), never tier identity => Pro and Family produce identical navigation output. Matches F10's 'Contract forward-compatible; product divergence would require a tier dimension.'

2) The Pro==Family-for-nav divergence that DOES exist is in subscription/billing UI, not navigation. BUG-899 rule (only Free/Plus surfaced as upgrade options; Family/Pro hidden from public upsell) lives in apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_subscription/constants.ts:6-16 ("BUG-899: Only Free and Plus are surfaced to end-users. Family/Pro tiers exist server-side but their store SKUs are not approved for public listing") plus BUG-917 read-only append (constants.ts:26-47, tier-helpers getTiersToCompare). The `tier === 'family' || tier === 'pro'` predicate at more/index.tsx:45-46 and subscription.tsx:121 only gates `useFamilySubscription()` hook enablement (a billing data fetch), not a nav gate. So Pro/Family identity matters only on the billing surface. Confirms 'Product/billing decision, not navigation.'

3) Spec corroboration: docs/specs/2026-05-21-navigation-contract.md:136 declares contract input `tier: 'free' | 'plus' | 'family' | 'pro' | null` — tier dimension is in the type but the spec assigns it no nav-divergent semantics; family surfaces are capability-driven, not tier-driven.

CITATION DRIFT (does not change verdict): C12 inventory row's described entity — a BUG-899 doc block at subscription.tsx:70-77 — is gone from that location (grep 'BUG-899' returns zero hits in subscription.tsx; L70-77 are now imports + an unrelated SubscriptionContent/ChildPaywall JSDoc). The BUG-899 doc relocated to _subscription/constants.ts:6-31. The 'actual gate' pointer more/index.tsx:71 drifted to more/index.tsx:45-46 / subscription.tsx:121, and that predicate gates hook-enable, not an upgrade-UI tier filter (which lives in constants.ts/tier-helpers.ts). These are inventory-row pointer rots, not a change to F10's product-vs-navigation conclusion.
recommendation: Keep F10 status as "Product/billing decision, not navigation." — it holds. Verified: navigation-contract.ts (apps/mobile/src/lib/navigation-contract.ts:65-70, 220-221, 260) carries tier/effectiveAccessTier as input but never discriminates pro-vs-family; gates key off effectiveAccessTier-presence and familyCapable only. The Pro==Family divergence is confined to subscription/billing UI (BUG-899/BUG-917). Fix the C12 inventory row's stale citations: move the BUG-899 doc-block reference from subscription.tsx:70-77 to apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/_subscription/constants.ts:6-16 (BUG-917 append at :26-47 / tier-helpers getTiersToCompare), and correct the tier-predicate pointer from more/index.tsx:71 to more/index.tsx:45-46 + subscription.tsx:121, noting it gates useFamilySubscription() enablement (billing fetch), not an upgrade-UI filter. Set severity Informational/Low (tracking note for a future tier dimension; no nav bug today).

claimedStatus: Implementation detail of the contract hook, captured in spec's "Failure Modes".
evidence:
The claimed status says the memoization rule is "captured in spec's Failure Modes" — it is NOT. The Failure Modes table in docs/specs/2026-05-21-navigation-contract.md:521-539 has eleven rows (dead Recaps tab, mode-switch failures, proxy exit, etc.) but ZERO entry about hook memoization, array-reference stability, or TanStack structural sharing. A grep for "memo|referenti|array ref|stable.*input|structural" across the spec AND both finalization plans (docs/_archive/plans/done/2026-05-23-navigation-contract-finalization.md, 2026-05-24-...phase-6-completion-plan.md) returns nothing. So the documentation claim is false.

The underlying concern is also a real, unguarded pattern in current source. The contract hook keys its useMemo on the raw profiles array reference: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-navigation-contract.ts:73-103 — `resolveNavigationContract({ ..., profiles, ... })` with dependency array `[activeProfile, profiles, parentProxy.isParentProxy, mode, role, ...]` (profiles at line 94). `profiles` originates from apps/mobile/src/lib/profile.ts:236-239: `const profilesQuery = useProfiles(); const profiles = useMemo(() => profilesQuery.data ?? [], [profilesQuery.data]);` — memoized only on `profilesQuery.data` identity, with a `?? []` fallback. apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-profiles.ts:13-42 is a bare useQuery with no `select` transform and no custom referential stabilization. Reference stability across refetches rests entirely on TanStack's default structuralSharing, which only de-dupes when the payload is deeply equal; the contract is NOT memoized on a stable derived signature (e.g. profile ids + isOwner). No guard test asserts referential stability of the contract — grep for "memoiz|referential|same reference|rerender" across the navigation-contract test files (use-navigation-contract.test.ts, navigation-contract*.test.ts) returns nothing.
recommendation: Mark F12 stale and correct two things: (1) Drop the "captured in spec's Failure Modes" claim — it is not in the spec. Either add a real Failure Modes row ("Contract re-resolves on every profiles refetch | new array reference churns useMemo | needless re-render / potential effect loops downstream | memoize on a stable signature: profile ids + isOwner + activeProfileId") or stop asserting documentation coverage. (2) Note the code reality: use-navigation-contract.ts:94 keys the contract memo on the raw profiles array (profile.ts:238 `profilesQuery.data ?? []`), so it relies on TanStack structuralSharing rather than a derived stable signature, and there is no guard test. Recommended fix if treated as live: change the memo dep from `profiles` to a stable key like `profiles.map(p => p.id+p.isOwner).join(',')` (or pass that signature into resolveNavigationContract), and add a rerender-stability test. Suggested severity LOW (perf/correctness-of-render, not a security/data gate).
